TextENGNew: There were no streetlights in this area, so just running through the darkness was frightening.
The moon may have lit the sky, but it did nothing to illuminate my feet.
The only thing lighting my path was the thin, unreliable beam of my flashlight.
The flashlight told me the ground ahead was a flat gravel road,@k but everything beyond its beam was unbelievably dark.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...The novelistic expression ``like coal tar had been poured over everything`` was no exaggeration at all, I thought.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: At any moment, I might trip over an empty can or a stone.

Terrified of that possibility, I swung the narrow beam desperately to confirm the safety of each step—yet I still couldn't feel at ease. Before I knew it, my pace had dropped from a run to barely more than a brisk walk.
I couldn't run because it was too dark to see my footing—because I was afraid of falling?
But even during the day, I didn't watch my feet while running.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I ran around with Rena and Mion all the time, laughing and playing.
Even in broad daylight, I never actually watched where I stepped.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Without the slightest thought that I might trip, I used to dash along roads just like this one.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: This might be exactly what Rena was going through right now.
For Rena... the light illuminating the darkness of her suspicion was far too faint.
And the darkness of the delusion she had convinced herself of was growing ever blacker, until she was too afraid to walk even familiar roads without crawling.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...She fell under the spell of those ridiculous scrapbooks.@k Some people might laugh at that.
But those scrapbooks... were not ordinary.
They had a malicious cunning—skillfully beguiling their readers' minds and exploiting the cracks in their hearts.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: That malice had captured even my mind for a single night.
...And that was precisely why I understood how insidious they were.
You know how there are classic urban legends—Nostradamus's great prophecy, the Purple Mirror, that sort of thing?

They pile up plausible-sounding lies so thoroughly that if you're just listening from the side, they seem utterly absurd.@k ...But once you get drawn in, somehow you can never quite shake them from your mind.

In another ten or twenty years, it would be 1999.@k The great prophecy that the world would end in July of that year was common knowledge, and the networks irresponsibly fanned the flames, so every now and then someone took it deathly seriously.
...You know, they'd say Nostradamus's prophecy foretold a nuclear war, and some people built bomb shelters in their backyards with their own money,
or tried to have themselves cryogenically preserved before the deadline so they could sleep through the radiation until the future. There were actually people doing these things in complete earnest.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: The people doing it were dead serious, no question.
But if you think about it calmly, there's no way a war would break out in 1999.

The East-West cold war structure certainly made people imagine a final war, but in reality it was a state of equilibrium maintained by mutually pointed nuclear arsenals—neither side had anything to gain from starting a nuclear conflict.

...``That country might actually do it—they'd destroy the world just to impose their ideology...``@k That paranoid refrain of ``that country is crazy enough to do it`` was identical to how ``the Sonozaki family`` functioned in Takano-san's scrapbooks.

Yes—in every grand delusion of this kind, there was always some country or political party or aliens,@k a faceless, incomprehensible hypothetical enemy, and the convenient assumption that ``they'd be capable of anything.``
What did Takano-san actually know about the Sonozaki family?
Nothing! She couldn't possibly know anything!
changeReason: Different phrasing conveys a noticeably different meaning.

TextENGNew: She was just dragging in history from the old Onigafuchi Village days and spouting irresponsible claims that the Sonozaki family was still scheming in the shadows today, plotting terrible conspiracies.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: You heard this kind of thing in every so-called prophecy.

The president of some country was plotting world domination, some past dictator was secretly still alive, and his remnants were continuing their conspiracy from an underground base in Antarctica—wasn't it exactly the same kind of nonsense?!
...Normally, Rena would have laughed off a story this stupid without a second thought.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: But... the timing happened to be terrible.
She had killed Rina and Teppei... and their bodies had just been discovered by us. It was an extremely fragile time for her.
We had been watching over her as she gradually recovered from that emotional shock.
...But before she could recover—before her mind could build up its defenses—a devastating poison had invaded Rena and spread through her entire being.
When you're sick and have a weakened immune system, it's easy to get another disease. It's just like that.
changeReason: Reverted: shifted: TextENGNew says "If only we'd been at her side" when JPN is about illness weakening immunity

TextENGNew: ...Yeah, she's just a little sick right now.
When you're very sick, in bed with a high fever, you sometimes feel like you might die from it.
changeReason: Reverted: shifted: TextENGNew says "I sprinted through the darkness" when JPN is about high fever

TextENGNew: But from a doctor's perspective, that would be the height of exaggeration.
The night road was dark and difficult to run on, so I repeatedly tripped and fell.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: But I didn't care. I'd be fine even if I twisted an ankle—I just got up again and kept running.
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: I crossed the bridge, and the road turned from paved to gravel.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: More importantly, if she keeps dawdling, the police might catch her!
From the direction of the highway, I could hear patrol cars' sirens wailing at intervals.
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: Getting stopped for questioning would be trouble, so each time I ducked out of sight and waited for them to pass.
I probably passed by two of them.
Along the way, I passed near Rena's house and noticed an unfamiliar car parked there. It was obvious they were lying in wait for her return.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Mion had apparently ordered the Sonozaki family to shelter Rena, but... honestly, it was a delicate situation.
After all, Rena believed the Sonozaki family was trying to kill her.
But if they didn't take her in, the police would catch her sooner or later.
And if that happened... ultimately, the rest of us who helped bury the bodies would be dragged into it too!
...I didn't know how the police had identified Rena as a suspect.
It was probably just circumstantial evidence and they didn't have certainty.
...But given the scale of their mobilization, they must have gotten hold of something close to conclusive...
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Rena, you idiot...!!
This is no time to play around with Takano-san's scrapbooks!@k The police are after you...!!
It was so dark I wasn't sure this was the right place.
I steeled myself and carefully made my way down the slope of scrap, illuminating my footing as I went.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: I wanted to call out Rena's name, but there was no telling if police searching for her might be nearby.
When I rounded the mountain of garbage, I spotted a light.@k ...There she was!
I rushed toward her, kicking through the junk as I went.
Rena was standing in front of the door of her hideout car, bracing herself with a menacing hatchet at the ready.
Of course she was.@k My clumsy, noisy descent must have given me away completely.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``...Keiichi-kun this time?@k@vS09/02/170201153. Ahaha, it's quite the night for visitors.``
``Someone else has been here?``
I swept my light around the area, but I couldn't see anyone other than Rena.
``This place isn't safe anymore, so I was just about to relocate.@k@vS09/02/170201155. ...I don't want you to know where I'm going, so don't follow me.``
``R-Rena?!@k@vS09/01/170100823. What happened to you?! Those wounds!@k@vS09/01/170100824. You're covered in blood!!``
changeReason: Old asks about 'your neck' specifically; new asks about general 'wounds' and says 'covered in blood'--different body part and severity described.

TextENGNew: ``...It's settled down now, so I'm fine.@k@vS09/02/170201157. Though I don't know when it might flare up again.``
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: The area around Rena's neck was bright red, as though someone suffering from a skin disease had scratched and clawed at it in a frenzy.
``Just wiping it with a towel won't be enough...@k@vS09/01/170100826. Come to my house for now!@k@vS09/01/170100827. We need to disinfect that properly and...``
``...The wound itself isn't serious.@k@vS09/02/170201159. And this isn't caused by the wound.@k@vS09/02/170201160. ...It's already... too late.``
changeReason: Old says 'It isn't an injury'; new says 'The wound itself isn't serious. And this isn't caused by the wound'--new provides a different explanation distinguishing wound from underlying condition.

TextENGNew: ``Too late?@k@vS09/01/170100829. ...What do you mean?``

``It seems like I was made to ingest whatever it was that killed Tomitake-san, without my knowing.@k@vS09/02/170201162. ...Not through something as crude as an injection.@k@vS09/02/170201163. It was probably mixed into food... and given to me by someone close.``
changeReason: Old reorders the JPN content (drug method before drug source); new follows the JPN order more faithfully. Both convey the same information but in different sequence.

TextENGNew: ``Remember?@k@vS09/02/170201165. The other day, Mii-chan divided up her tamagoyaki and gave us each one piece?@k@vS09/02/170201166. That was it... That's how I was poisoned!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Rena recalled Mion's tamagoyaki and kicked the gravel with unmistakable hatred.
...She had literally kicked away Mion's goodwill.
``Listen, Rena.@k@vS09/01/170100831. ...Do you honestly believe Mion would do something like that?``
Rena blinked at me in bewilderment.@k ...Then a hint of malice crept into her eyes.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: I already told him the Sonozaki family is the root of all evil—what is he even saying?@k That was what her gaze said.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``First of all...@k@vS09/01/170100833. That day with the tamagoyaki—Mion didn't hand them out one per person.``
Mion had placed the tamagoyaki, cut into five pieces, in front of everyone and said help yourselves.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...And Rena had been the first to pick one—she chose the piece at the end, squealing about how cute the little spiral was before popping it into her mouth.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``That's not right.@k@vS09/02/170201171. Mii-chan deliberately made me eat the poisoned piece.``
``...Rena.@k@vS09/01/170100835. ...Don't rewrite your memories to suit your story...@k@vS09/01/170100836. She laid them out on the lid of the container, all lined up on a little platter...``
``She didn't lay them out...!@k@vS09/02/170201173. She handed them out one per person!``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``...Has that ever happened before?@k@vS09/01/170100838. We always share our lunches buffet-style, everyone picking from each other's boxes...@k@vS09/01/170100839. We've never once politely portioned things out saying 'this one's yours'... have we...``
I could see Rena's eyes flooding with hatred in real time.@k ...To the Rena standing before me, I was the one spouting delusions.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: In her mind, being poisoned was already established fact, and she had ``decided`` it was the tamagoyaki.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...That was why she was rejecting me—because my account contradicted her truth.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ...I hadn't come here to make Rena angry.
...Realizing that pressing further would only lead to a fruitless argument, I dropped the subject.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``Anyway.@k@vS09/01/170100841. ...You said this place isn't safe anymore—where are you planning to go?``
``...Who knows.@k@vS09/02/170201176. I'll find somewhere.``
``You can't just find a place to sleep that easily...!@k@vS09/01/170100843. Come to my house.``

``...I appreciate it, but I'll have to decline.@k@vS09/02/170201178. Sonozaki operatives are combing the entire village.@k@vS09/02/170201179. ...I don't want to think you'd betray me and sell me out, Keiichi-kun... but I don't think you could hide me either.``
``The Sonozaki operatives aren't the ones searching for you—it's the opposite.@k@vS09/01/170100845. It's the police.``
``...?@k@vS09/02/170201181. Why?@k@vS09/02/170201182. Ooishi-san is on my side... He said he'd leave me alone...``
``Probably... it leaked somehow. That you killed Rina and Teppei.``
I was relieved that our conversation had finally connected—but Rena's self-deprecating laugh struck me as slightly off, and I quickly realized we were still talking past each other.
``...Do you know how the police figured out about those killings?``
``...No... How would I know...``
At the very least, no one had witnessed the actual killings.
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: When we went deep into the mountains to hide the bodies, everything had gone smoothly, and the burial site was perfect.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: On top of that, Mion had apparently reburied them in a more secure location afterward.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: So there was absolutely no way those bodies would ever be found.
``This evening.@k@vS09/02/170201186. ...I went back to where I buried those two.@k@vS09/02/170201187. And... the bodies were gone.``
changeReason: Old builds suspense with 'Guess what I found...'; new directly reveals 'the bodies were gone'--different narrative approach, new reveals the key information upfront.

TextENGNew: That's bad, I thought.
...That's right.
Rena didn't know that Mion had moved the bodies.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: So she must have been convinced they were still buried there...!
``They were gone.@k@vS09/02/170201189. The bodies.@k@vS09/02/170201190. Someone dug them up and took them away.``
``W-Wait, Rena!@k@vS09/01/170100851. ...That's because—um...!``

``No one would happen to dig in that spot by coincidence.@k@vS09/02/170201192. ...Besides, I chose that location myself.@k@vS09/02/170201193. ...So the only people who could have found the bodies are the ones who followed me.``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``Y-Yeah, you're right about that...@k@vS09/01/170100853. So, um, the thing is...``
``I know who did it.@k@vS09/02/170201195. It was Mii-chan.@k@vS09/02/170201196. ...She dug up the bodies and handed them to the police to make sure I couldn't ally with the police myself.``
changeReason: Old says Mion wanted 'insurance' to prevent Rena from working with police; new says Mion 'dug up the bodies and handed them to the police'--fundamentally different accusation about Mion's actions.

TextENGNew: ``...Not content with sending the Sonozaki family after me, now she's using the police too.@k@vS09/02/170201198. ...They must be getting desperate.``
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ``There's no way Mion would do something like that!``
``She did!!@k@vS09/02/170201200. Mii-chan sold me out!``
``...No, she didn't.@k@vS09/01/170100856. Mion and all of us—we're friends.@k@vS09/01/170100857. Even if the entire world turned against you, I know for a fact she'd be on your side.``
``Mion would never betray you—not even if every drop of seawater evaporated and the world itself was ending!``
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: ``...Why are you taking Mii-chan's side?``
``I'm not! It's the other way around!@k@vS09/01/170100860. Why do you suspect Mion that much?!``
``Isn't it obvious?@k@vS09/02/170201203. The fatal truth Miyo-san uncovered threatens the believers' grand plan—``
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ``Stop it! Stop it!!@k@vS09/01/170100861. ...Ah, that came out too harsh.@k@vS09/01/170100862. I'm sorry, just listen to me for a second.@k@vS09/01/170100863. ...Listen, the reason those bodies were gone is because Mion dug them up.``
``See?! That just proves it!!!``
``Let me finish!!@k@vS09/01/170100865. ...Listen. The reason Mion dug up the bodies was because we found out that burial site wasn't safe.``
``The Forestry Agency had been planning to do logging in that entire area this summer.@k@vS09/01/170100867. So Mion!@k@vS09/01/170100868. She dug up the bodies to protect you, Rena!!``
``Pfft... Ahahahahaha, ahahahahaha!``
Rena's laughter was pure contempt.
My words weren't reaching her heart at all...
``That's obviously just something Mii-chan made up on the spot, isn't it?!@k@vS09/02/170201207. It's just her dirty trick to look like the good girl in front of you, Keiichi-kun!``

``Come on...!!@k@vS09/01/170100870. Then what should she have done?!@k@vS09/01/170100871. Knowing the Forestry Agency might find them this summer, you think she should've just left them there?!@k@vS09/01/170100872. There's no way she could do that!``
``Why can't you see that this so-called logging plan was Mii-chan's fabrication from the very start?!``
``You're the one who can't see, Rena!!!``
When I shouted at her, Rena's expression went cold, as if all warmth had drained from her in an instant.
...Then she stared at me, appraising me—what had this man come here for?
``...I came to wake you up.@k@vS09/01/170100875. You've been possessed by some terrible dream because of Takano-san's scrapbooks.``
``...At first I thought it was an interesting story too, but it's gone way past the point of being a joke.@k@vS09/01/170100877. ...You're just a little sick right now.``
changeReason: Old says 'they're making you sick' (external cause); new says 'You're just a little sick right now' (internal/neutral cause)--different attribution of illness.

TextENGNew: ``...Oh, the Rika impostor said the same thing—that I'm sick.@k@vS09/02/170201211. And it's true—the maggot disease has already started.``

``No, no, no, that's not what I mean!@k@vS09/01/170100879. Just listen to me!@k@vS09/01/170100880. ...Why don't you lie down and rest for a while?@k@vS09/01/170100881. Honestly, it'd be safest to hide at Mion's house.``

``They can hide you from the police too.@k@vS09/01/170100883. ...But if you really can't trust Mion, then my house is fine.@k@vS09/01/170100884. Either way, curling up in a place like this where you can't rest is no good for your state of mind!``
``Let's go to my house first.@k@vS09/01/170100886. We can have something cold to drink and cool your head a little, yeah?@k@vS09/01/170100887. Let's talk it out calmly.``
``What are you saying, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS09/02/170201213. ...Are you trying to tell me I've lost my mind?``

``The ones who have lost their minds are all of you!!@k@vS09/02/170201215. You've been taken over by the parasites and are being controlled without even knowing it!@k@vS09/02/170201216. Keiichi-kun, you think you're just a bystander, but you're actually one of the victims!!``
``The cunning thing about them is that you can't even notice it.@k@vS09/02/170201218. I don't blame you for that.@k@vS09/02/170201219. ...If I were in your shoes, I'm sure I'd be the same.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``'The ones who are crazy are all of you'—is that what you're saying...?@k@vS09/01/170100890. ...So Rena, you think the entire village is insane and you're the only sane one...?!``
``It might not be just Hinamizawa.@k@vS09/02/170201221. ...Their clutches may have already extended to all of Shishibone City... or even the entire prefecture!``
``But I alone am right!@k@vS09/02/170201223. Thanks to Miyo-san's scrapbooks, I was able to see the truth!@k@vS09/02/170201224. Keiichi-kun, didn't you realize it too?!``
``Wait, Rena, c'mon, wait...!!@k@vS09/01/170100892. If you're saying everyone in the world besides you is crazy—that means you're admitting...``
changeReason: Old completes the argument: 'That's the same thing as saying you're the only one who's crazy'; new trails off at 'you're admitting...' leaving the conclusion unstated.

TextENGNew: ``The world hasn't changed one bit from the start—there's nothing wrong with it.@k@vS09/01/170100895. And it sure as hell isn't Rena's enemy.@k@vS09/01/170100896. It's impossible for you alone to be right and the whole world to be your enemy!``
``That means... the only enemy... is yourself!``
``Poor Keiichi-kun.@k@vS09/02/170201226. ...But it can't be helped.@k@vS09/02/170201227. Miyo-san's truth isn't something everyone can accept.``
changeReason: Old includes 'Even she wasn't sure about them' (Takano's own uncertainty); new omits this detail, just saying her truth isn't easy to accept.

TextENGNew: ``So I understand that you can't accept it, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS09/02/170201230. ...That's why I'm going to fight hard to save people like you.@k@vS09/02/170201231. ...I'll definitely find a way to stop those things!``
You... you idiot... you idiot...!!
``Poor Keiichi-kun``?@k You fool, you total fool!!
How can you not see it?! How can you still believe such an incoherent, ridiculous story?!
And why do you keep twisting everything to fit that delusion every time something happens?!
``...Miyo-san and Rika-chan, and now me—spares have already been prepared for all of us.@k@vS09/02/170201233. And the moment I'm eliminated, the swap will happen.``
``Huh?!@k@vS09/01/170100899. Spares?!@k@vS09/01/170100900. Are you seriously hearing yourself right now?!``

``Forget it.@k@vS09/02/170201235. I'll tell you the real truth, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS09/02/170201236. The real truth that I haven't shared with you yet.@k@vS09/02/170201237. You see, the true identity of the parasites is... aliens.``
``A-Aliens...?!``

``Yes.@k@vS09/02/170201239. Oyashiro-sama's true identity is an alien, obviously.@k@vS09/02/170201240. The descriptions of descending from heaven match accounts of extraterrestrial contact found all around the world.``

``Whenever a great civilization arises, its founders always come from space!@k@vS09/02/170201242. They visit Earth bearing superior wisdom, and they instill advanced knowledge in the immature humans of this planet!``
...Wait, wait, wait, wait... You... You can't be serious, Rena.
At least... the parasite theory was more interesting to listen to...
That's just... completely absurd...
changeReason: Different phrasing conveys a noticeably different meaning.

TextENGNew: But Rena was unbelievably serious, and... she pitied me for being unable to accept it.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``They've controlled communities through bodily parasitism.@k@vS09/02/170201244. But occasionally, a perceptive human is born who tries to expose the truth of their control.@k@vS09/02/170201245. Their solution? They silence them through 'divine punishments' and 'mysterious disappearances'!``

changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

changeReason: Old says the copy 'admitted that it came from space'; new says 'She proved she wasn't the real Rika-chan by her own admission'--different claims about what the alleged copy admitted.

TextENGNew: You... you idiot...!!!!
I strode up to Rena and slapped the top of her head.

TextENGNew: ``...Snap out of it!!``

``If you honestly believe what you're saying—that aliens are invading the earth—@k@vS09/01/170100910. then even if the whole world refuses to believe you, I will.@k@vS09/01/170100911. ...Because I'm your friend, and the very first person on your side!!``

TextENGNew: ``But if that's the case... then you listen to me too.@k@vS09/01/170100913. Listen to your friend and ally.@k@vS09/01/170100914. ...Those scrapbooks of Takano-san's are fabricated nonsense!!@k@vS09/01/170100915. It's true—Takano-san is an occult fanatic.``
``She's just reinterpreting Hinamizawa's old history in various ways for fun, writing up eccentric theories and showing them to people.``
``That's why there aren't just parasite theories—there are absurd ones about UFOs and subterranean people and all sorts of other nonsense!``
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ``So the scrapbooks Rena read were nothing more than Takano-san's latest and greatest work of fiction!@k@vS09/01/170100919. You know how she liked to toy with people, right?``
``You were so earnest about listening that she got a kick out of it and fed you an outrageous story, that's all!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``...If they were just fiction, then why was Miyo-san killed?@k@vS09/02/170201256. And the autopsy said she'd been dead for twenty-four hours, so why was she seen alive on the night of the festival?!``

``What about Tomitake-san's cause of death?@k@vS09/02/170201258. What about the Sonozaki family?@k@vS09/02/170201259. What about Hinamizawa's legends?@k@vS09/02/170201260. What about Oyashiro-sama, what about Rika-chan?@k@vS09/02/170201261. All of it, fiction?@k@vS09/02/170201262. Are you saying the entire world is lying just to deceive us?``
``Stop forcing connections between unrelated things!!@k@vS09/01/170100922. That's a textbook case of megalomania!!@k@vS09/01/170100923. I'm begging you, listen to me!``
changeReason: Old diagnoses 'paranoia' and says 'You're just tired'; new diagnoses 'megalomania' and says 'I'm begging you, listen to me'--different psychological terms and different appeals.

TextENGNew: ``So please, let's just rest for a while.@k@vS09/01/170100926. All I'm offering is a room where you can relax, a futon, and a meal.@k@vS09/01/170100927. I promise I won't do a single thing you wouldn't like.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Please! Just please!!@k@vS09/01/170100929. Believe what I'm saying, if only for right now!!!``
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: ``My account is far more logical, and I have plenty of evidence and reasoning to back it up.@k@vS09/02/170201264. Yet all you can do is insist that I believe you.@k@vS09/02/170201265. Between the two of us, which one is more convincing?``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``Yeah, it's obvious.@k@vS09/01/170100931. Me!!!@k@vS09/01/170100932. I'm the one who's right!``
``H-How... How can you say that with such confidence?``

``Because it's simple.@k@vS09/01/170100934. Because I'm your friend.@k@vS09/01/170100935. A friend never betrays you.@k@vS09/01/170100936. Even if they don't have proof or evidence or logic—they're always on your side.``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: There was a heavy thud.@k ...It was the sound of Rena dropping her hatchet.
Rena clutched her head with both hands and swayed slightly.
...She seemed to be muttering something inaudible, as if arguing with herself.
...He can't show me a single shred of proof or reasoning, and he expects me to just believe him?!
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: What kind of ridiculous thing to say—who does this Maebara Keiichi think he is...?!
But... maybe Keiichi-kun is actually right... and this is all just my misunderstanding...?
Oh, what a wonderful proposition—one I've dreamed of so many times.
That Miyo-san's scrapbooks are nothing more than a delusional notebook, and the parasites don't exist.
...That I've simply been stringing coincidences together and interpreting them in the worst possible way...
If only that were true—oh, if only that were true...!!
Don't falter, Ryuugu Rena. Stay cool, Ryuugu Rena...!!
This man's words carry a poison—a poison that preys on my heart's weakness, my craving for peace!
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Everything is already clear—alien invaders have been using this village, once called Onigafuchi, as a base for their conquest of Earth since ancient times...
Keiichi-kun has been parasitized and subjugated—he's nothing but a victim...!
But... that day, when Keiichi-kun seized my wrist, there was no malice in that strength!!
That powerful grip, reaching out to save a friend—I still can't forget it!!
In this maze of confusion and delusion, I've collapsed, unable to find the exit.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ...How many times have I prayed for someone to come help me?!
And now, my prayer has been answered—Keiichi-kun is reaching out his hand to me again, in this same place!!
Have you forgotten, Ryuugu Rena? This man claims to be your friend, but he hasn't told you everything about himself.
Remember—you heard it from Ooishi-san...
The police investigated.
Because I suspected my friends, Ooishi looked into every one of them, Keiichi-kun included.
And that's when... I learned about what Keiichi-kun had done before he moved here...!!
He's all surface—underneath, he's an aberration! I can't trust him, I can't trust him!!
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: So what?!
I don't know anything about Keiichi-kun from before he moved here!!
I did plenty of bad things back in Ibaraki before I moved too!
Things I couldn't laugh off. Things that can't be forgiven.
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: I seriously injured someone, I smashed every window in the school—I was such a delinquent I got suspended!!
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: And did they hold that against me?
Of course not!
Ryuugu Rena is Ryuugu Rena... and even though Ryuugu Reina back in Ibaraki... did terrible things... they forgave me...!
Ahahaha, ahahahahahaha!!!

They don't forgive—they could never forgive!@k Of course not!@k Maebara Keiichi only believes I'm a proper, ladylike girl because he doesn't know about Reina.@k If he found out—who knows what would happen!!
changeReason: Old uses singular 'He won\'t forgive me' where JPN says 許さないよ許すわけないよ (more emphatic/general 'they won\'t/can\'t forgive'); new captures the emphatic repetition better.

TextENGNew: It would be fine even if he knew. He wouldn't be afraid of me...!
Rena is Rena! Reina was someone from another town, another life!!
Keiichi-kun is the same—what happened before he moved here doesn't matter! We're even on that score!!
``...A-Are you okay, Rena...``
``...Don't touch me, you filthy—``
changeReason: Old says 'Don't touch me'; new adds 'you filthy--' with an unfinished insult, adding hostility not present in original.

TextENGNew: My outstretched hand was slapped away.

``This whole time... all I hear is 'friend, friend, friend' over and over...@k@vS09/02/170201271. Believe you because we're friends?@k@vS09/02/170201272. Because we're friends, you're more right than the entire world...?@k@vS09/02/170201273. Now that's the real delusion...!``
``Say what you want.@k@vS09/01/170100940. I am your friend.@k@vS09/01/170100941. That's why everything I say will always be for your sake.``
changeReason: Old includes 'Don't doubt me!! I know I'm right!!' which is omitted in new; new ends with a softer 'That's why everything I say will always be for your sake.'

TextENGNew: ``...Pfft... Heh heh heh heh!!``
``Oh, really...?@k@vS09/02/170201276. Then, may I ask you something?``
``Go ahead. Ask me anything.``
``Friends... don't have secrets from each other, right?``
``Of course not.@k@vS09/01/170100946. I have nothing to hide.``
``Liar.``
``...I'm not lying!``
``Aaahahahahahahaha!!@k@vS09/02/170201281. Ahahahahaha!!``
``Wh-What's so funny?!``
``Ahahaha... You think you've been hiding it, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS09/02/170201283. Heh heh heh!``
``I know everything.``
``I know what you were doing right before you moved here...``
...It felt as though frost was erupting from every pore in my body.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: My entire being froze, and even my thoughts froze with it.
...That's impossible, that's impossible... How... do you know...?!
changeReason: Old adds 'she knows' with emphasis on pronoun; new uses 'you know' (direct address). JPN 知ッテルンダヨ is ambiguous but both are reasonable interpretations.

TextENGNew: ``I had Ooishi-san look into it.@k@vS09/02/170201287. Everyone.@k@vS09/02/170201288. ...And it turns out, before you moved here, you were quite something, weren't you, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS09/02/170201289. Heh heh heh!``
``S-Stop... That was... It's not what you think...``
``Sounds like you were quite the bad boy.@k@vS09/02/170201291. I should probably tell Satoko-chan and Rika-chan too.@k@vS09/02/170201292. ...I'm sure they'd be fascinated.``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``No, it's... it's not like that... It was just... a fluke...``

``Serial attacks on random pedestrians with a model gun.@k@vS09/02/170201294. Wow, that's impressive—almost as impactful as the Hinamizawa serial mysterious deaths.@k@vS09/02/170201295. Quite the little criminal, weren't you?``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``That's not... That was... I swear...``
``What's 'not' about it?@k@vS09/02/170201297. For weeks, you targeted random pedestrians and shot at them with a model gun as they passed by. A terrible crime.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Someone got hit in the eye and was seriously injured, right?@k@vS09/02/170201300. Luckily there were no lasting effects.@k@vS09/02/170201301. But that's just luck, not your doing.``
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ``Carrying a weapon like a model gun, indiscriminately stalking people for fun—how revolting.@k@vS09/02/170201303. What pleasure could you have possibly gotten from that?``
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ``But it must have been fun, since you kept at it for so long.@k@vS09/02/170201305. And then you couldn't bear the guilt anymore and turned yourself in with your parents.@k@vS09/02/170201306. You can't even take responsibility without Mommy and Daddy?``

Juvenile law was kind to you, wasn't it?@k@vS09/02/170201308. It prevented your name from being disclosed to the public or the victims.@k@vS09/02/170201309. I bet your father spent a lot of money to protect you.@k@vS09/02/170201310_1. He paid a lot of compensation to settle the case out of court, and he had you placed on probation.
changeReason: Reverted: truncated: cuts off Rena speech about juvenile law, settlement money, and probation

TextENGNew: ``So once things cooled down, you just threw money at the problem and moved far away to start fresh.@k@vS09/02/170201312. Must be nice, being wealthy.@k@vS09/02/170201313. Must be so nice.``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``...It's not... It's not like that... ahhh...``
``Why did you hide that all this time?@k@vS09/02/170201315. 'I was just studying all the time at my old school'?@k@vS09/02/170201316. That's a bald-faced lie!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``I-I'm sorry...@k@vS09/01/170100954. It wasn't exactly a lie—``
``Friends don't keep secrets, right?@k@vS09/02/170201318. Right?!``
``Then you are NOT my friend!!``
...The blade of those words struck the back of my skull... and I bled from my eyes.@k The blood was clear... and tasted of salt.
``I also think you're suspicious because of your past.@k@vS09/02/170201321. Don't think you've been fooling me!!``
The remorse from that time came surging back.
A regret I could never repent enough for—something I wanted to forget but never could—that stupid, senseless mistake.
...Why... Why did that incident... have to surface here and now...?
I had apologized, accepted everything... and by moving away, I was supposed to have put it all behind me...
That's why I'd thrown myself into my new life in Hinamizawa with such determination—I'd sworn to make a clean, fresh start...
All of it... was ruined.
...No matter where I go... I'm a criminal.
...The stress of exam prep, I was frustrated, anyone would do—ahhh, what was the point of something so idiotic?
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: There was no point. There was nothing to it.
I just wanted to be spoiled—that was all.
changeReason: Old says 'wanted to get someone's attention' (seeking notice); new says 'wanted to be spoiled' (seeking indulgence)--different motivations described per JPN 甘えたかった (wanting to be pampered).

TextENGNew: ...I didn't have the courage to tell my parents to my face that I hated studying for exams... I wanted someone to scold me, so I resorted to such a childish method...
...I regretted it. I could swear on my life never to do anything so stupid again!
I'd accept the death penalty if I ever did it again!!
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: If there was some threshold of atonement that would earn forgiveness, I would reach it no matter what!!
But... no matter how deeply I regretted it, no matter how much I atoned... I would never be forgiven...
So... at the very least, with a fresh start in a new life... I wanted to pretend it had never happened.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: If someone told me that forgetting your sins was cowardly... I couldn't argue.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...A sin is a sin.
It can never be destroyed.
You have no choice but to carry it for the rest of your life.
I know that's the fate of a sinner.
...And I understand the logic that says if you don't like it, you shouldn't have sinned.
...Then how... how is someone who has sinned supposed to go on living...?
``Anyway.@k@vS09/02/170201323. You are not my friend.@k@vS09/02/170201324. ...I have no obligation to take orders from someone who isn't my friend.``
changeReason: Old includes 'I need you to leave me alone for a while. ...Okay?' which is omitted in new--the request for space is lost.

TextENGNew: Before I knew it, I was on my knees, head hanging.
I could see Rena picking up the hatchet she had dropped at her feet.@k ...If anything, I wished she would smash my skull with it.
That's right.
If I was going to be torn apart either way, being torn apart in body would be far better.
Compared to the pain of having my heart ripped open... having my skull split would be infinitely preferable.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: I had believed.
...No—I still believe.@k Even in this very moment, I believe.
I believe... that I am everyone's friend.
But... deep down, I've always had an inkling.
changeReason: Different phrasing conveys a noticeably different meaning.

TextENGNew: I want to believe only because I don't want to accept the truth.
My own voice, trying to convince myself, sounded so pathetically tearful... it was too absurd to bear...
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: And more tears... made my face an even greater mess...
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I... wasn't their friend after all.
I was a filthy criminal... and I had been hiding my dirty past.
I was afraid of being found out, so I hid it.
changeReason: Different phrasing conveys a noticeably different meaning.

TextENGNew: I had cornered others with the demand that friends don't keep secrets—and all along, I was the one hiding.@k I was hiding things too.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: What does my past... have to do with my daily life here...?
If I ever did something like that again in this village—attacking random people out of spite—then by all means, tear me apart!
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: But... I would never do something like that again!
I won't, so I just want to live a normal life...!!
Whatever happened before I moved here... what does that have to do with 'now'...?!
For instance... Rena moved here too, didn't she?
Even if I learned something had happened before she moved here...@k ...I wouldn't change the way I look at her...
I wouldn't, right?@k ...Right...?
Even if Rena had, say... beaten up some students, or smashed every window in the school... that was all back in Ibaraki.
She hadn't done anything like that in Hinamizawa... Rena was Rena—our irreplaceable friend...
So... even if I learned about Rena's past... I would never look at her... as something to be disgusted by...!
...That's a lie.@k ...It's a lie, a lie, a lie...!!!
If I learned that gentle, kind Rena had that kind of past... I would put distance between us.
I would be afraid, I would shrink back, and I would stop listening to her words.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Why?! It happened before she moved here—it's just hearsay, just a rumor!
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: And to me, Rena is the best friend, the best companion... the closest... ally I have!
...If our positions were reversed... would I have rejected Rena too...?
Then... Rena rejecting me... is perfectly natural...
So... what am I... supposed to do...?
I was the only one crying.
She shed no tears.
She looked at me as though gazing at something filthy.
And with a single, cold glance, she vanished into the darkness...
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: I... couldn't earn Rena's forgiveness.
...All I could do was stay there on my knees, endlessly, shedding tears... to atone for my sins...
